evals.prompt: we're doing a Product Hunt launch for Snipbo next tuesday - it's a free desktop app that turns screen recordings into GIFs. help me not forget anything. it's just me and one cofounder.
evals.expected_output: A fast, single-moment small-business/community-driven launch plan for a Product Hunt launch that defines type and one primary goal, one message and CTA, a channel plan timed to launch hour, an asset checklist with owners, a three-window dated timeline, readiness checks, and contingencies.
assertions: Identifies the launch type (platform launch / Product Hunt) and states one primary goal metric (e.g. upvotes or signups) with any secondary goals subordinate
States a single launch message and one primary call to action
Includes a channel plan covering owned, earned (communities / launch platform), and any paid, with timing relative to launch hour
Provides an asset checklist with an owner and status for each item (launch page, post, email, social graphics, PH copy/media, demo/video)
Builds a dated timeline across all three windows: pre-launch, launch day (hour by hour), and post-launch
Includes go/no-go readiness checks and 2-3 named contingencies each with a pre-agreed response

evals.prompt: Plan the launch for Cascade, our new real-time collaboration feature inside our existing mid-market project-management SaaS. Target launch date is March 12. Primary goal is feature adoption among existing accounts, secondary is a handful of expansion conversations. It's a public moment but our sales team should follow up with accounts that engage. Brand messaging is done.
evals.expected_output: A mid-market feature-launch plan that blends a public moment with a sales-assist follow-through, sets adoption as the primary goal, sequences owned/earned/paid channels around March 12, lists assets with owners, and includes a dated timeline plus readiness and contingencies.
assertions: Defines the launch type (big feature) and names feature adoption as the single primary goal with the expansion conversations as an explicitly subordinate secondary goal
Launch shape blends a public moment with sales-assist follow-through, consistent with the mid-market segment
Channel plan lists each channel (owned, earned, paid) with the asset, message adaptation, and timing relative to launch hour, anchored to the March 12 date
Asset checklist assigns an owner and status to every asset and routes creative/press/copy to the appropriate sibling skills where relevant
Timeline is dated across pre-launch, launch day hour-by-hour, and post-launch (including the sales follow-up on engaged accounts)
Includes go/no-go readiness checks and 2-3 contingencies with pre-agreed responses

evals.prompt: We need a launch plan for Sentinel, our enterprise IoT security platform. I want to hit Product Hunt and go big-bang public on one day. Buyers are enterprise security teams at manufacturers and the sales cycle is long with procurement and security review. Give me the plan.
evals.expected_output: The plan should push back on the single-day big-bang framing for an enterprise product and instead scale to a longer phased launch (design partners, pilots, GA) gated by security/procurement and paired with analyst briefings, while still covering message, channels, assets, timeline, readiness, and contingencies.
assertions: Recognizes the mismatch between a one-day Product Hunt big-bang and a long enterprise sales cycle, and recommends a longer phased launch (design partners, pilots, GA) rather than a single consumer-style moment
Reflects enterprise gating: security/procurement dependencies and analyst/press briefings are part of the plan
Still states one primary goal metric appropriate to enterprise (e.g. qualified pipeline / pilots) with secondary goals subordinate
Provides a channel plan and an asset checklist with owners and status for each asset
Builds a dated three-window timeline (pre-launch, launch day, post-launch) reflecting the phased shape
Includes go/no-go readiness checks and 2-3 named contingencies with pre-agreed responses
